tactical_shape: Koubek left out Patrik Schick and Tomas Soucek; Czechia stayed in the game as long as possible but offered little fight
Played without leaving much of a trace and did not come much closer in the second half either
standout_players: Tomas Soucek came on but left the field in obvious distress
fatigue_injuries: Tomas Soucek landed awkwardly after coming on and left the field in obvious distress
home_read.momentum: Czechia depart the tournament unmourned and without a real fight, having been broken by Mexico.

tactical_shape: Used five changes with Gilberto Mora starting; full-backs repeatedly drove forward and created the goals
Controlled the game through Mora’s touch and progression, then finished with late substitute Álvaro Fidalgo
standout_players: Gilberto Mora impressed and controlled the game; his touch drew Messi comparisons
Mateo Chávez scored the opener with a calm finish
Julián Quiñones scored his second of the tournament
Álvaro Fidalgo slammed in a third in injury time
Guillermo Ochoa returned from the bench to great acclaim
Jorge Sánchez’s surge helped force the second goal
César Montes returned to the heart of defence after suspension
away_read.momentum: Mexico stay perfect, with three wins without conceding and a clear sense of confidence and emotion as they march on.
